title: Phase 1: Minimal Sync (v0.1)
content:
# Phase 1: Minimal Sync (v0.1)

Parent issue tracking all Phase 1 work. Goal: Two peers sync a Y.Map in real-time over Nebula.

## Prerequisites
- Two machines with Nebula installed and connected
- Manually created Nebula certificates

## Success Criteria
- Run demo on two machines
- Edit Y.Map on machine A → appears on machine B within 1 second
- Bidirectional sync working

## Sub-issues
- [[i-proj]]: Project setup
- [[i-mesh]]: Minimal NebulaMesh
- [[i-chan]]: Minimal MessageChannel  
- [[i-yjs]]: Minimal YjsSyncProvider
- [[i-demo]]: Demo script

Implements [[s-6wrw]]

title: Minimal MessageChannel implementation
content:
# Minimal MessageChannel

Implement basic MessageChannel for typed P2P messaging.

## Scope (Phase 1 - Minimal)
- [ ] `MessageChannel<T>` generic class
- [ ] `open()` / `close()` lifecycle
- [ ] `send(peerId, message)` - unicast to specific peer
- [ ] `broadcast(message)` - send to all connected peers
- [ ] `on('message', handler)` - receive messages
- [ ] `on('error', handler)` - error handling
- [ ] JSON serialization for messages
- [ ] Channel name for namespacing

## NOT in scope (Phase 1)
- Offline queue (Phase 3)
- RPC request/response (Phase 3)
- Binary serialization (Phase 5)
- Retry logic (Phase 3)

## API
```typescript
const channel = mesh.createChannel<MyMessage>('my-channel')
await channel.open()

channel.on('message', (msg, from) => {
  console.log(`From ${from.id}:`, msg)
})

channel.broadcast({ type: 'hello' })
channel.send('bob', { type: 'direct' })
```

## Acceptance
- Messages sent arrive at destination
- Broadcast reaches all peers
- Type safety preserved

title: Minimal NebulaMesh implementation
content:
# Minimal NebulaMesh

Implement basic NebulaMesh class for peer connectivity over Nebula.

## Scope (Phase 1 - Minimal)
- [ ] `NebulaMesh` class with config
- [ ] `connect()` / `disconnect()` lifecycle
- [ ] TCP socket communication over Nebula IPs
- [ ] Hardcoded peer list (no discovery yet)
- [ ] `getPeers()` returns configured peers
- [ ] `getSelf()` returns local peer info
- [ ] `createChannel()` factory method
- [ ] Basic events: `peer:joined`, `peer:left`, `connected`, `disconnected`

## NOT in scope (Phase 1)
- Hub election (Phase 3)
- Peer discovery (Phase 3)
- Namespace registry (Phase 3)
- Health monitoring (Phase 3)

## API
```typescript
const mesh = new NebulaMesh({
  peerId: 'alice',
  nebulaIp: '10.42.0.10',
  peers: [
    { id: 'bob', nebulaIp: '10.42.0.11' }
  ]
})
await mesh.connect()
```

## Acceptance
- Can connect to peer over Nebula
- Events fire on connect/disconnect

Implements [[s-65f6]]

title: Phase 1 demo script and documentation
content:
# Demo Script

Create a working demo that proves Phase 1 functionality.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `examples/basic-sync.ts` demo script
- [ ] Document Nebula setup prerequisites
- [ ] Create test certificates for demo (manual process doc)
- [ ] Write README section for running demo

## Demo Script Behavior
```typescript
// examples/basic-sync.ts
// Run on two machines with Nebula connected

const mesh = new NebulaMesh({
  peerId: process.env.PEER_ID,
  nebulaIp: process.env.NEBULA_IP,
  peers: JSON.parse(process.env.PEERS)
})

await mesh.connect()
console.log('Connected to mesh')

const provider = new YjsSyncProvider(mesh, { namespace: 'demo' })
await provider.start()
console.log('Sync started')

const shared = provider.getMap<string>('demo-data')

// Watch for changes
shared.observe((event) => {
  console.log('Data changed:', Object.fromEntries(shared.entries()))
})

// Interactive: read from stdin, write to shared map
// Or: periodic updates for automated testing
```

## Documentation
- Prerequisites (Nebula install, cert setup)
- Step-by-step: run demo on machine A
- Step-by-step: run demo on machine B  
- Expected output
- Troubleshooting

## Acceptance
- Demo runs successfully on two machines
- Changes propagate bidirectionally
- Clear documentation for reproduction

title: Minimal YjsSyncProvider implementation
content:
# Minimal YjsSyncProvider

Implement basic YjsSyncProvider for CRDT-based state sync.

## Scope (Phase 1 - Minimal)
- [ ] `YjsSyncProvider` class extending `SyncProvider`
- [ ] Wraps `Y.Doc` instance
- [ ] `start()` - register namespace, open channel, begin sync
- [ ] `stop()` - cleanup
- [ ] Use y-protocols sync algorithm (sync-step-1, sync-step-2, update)
- [ ] Sync with peers on start
- [ ] Broadcast updates on local Y.Doc change
- [ ] `getMap()`, `getArray()`, `getText()` convenience methods
- [ ] `synced` / `syncing` state properties
- [ ] Events: `synced`, `syncing`, `error`, `update`

## NOT in scope (Phase 1)
- Persistence/snapshots (Phase 3)
- Awareness/presence (Phase 3)
- Hub-aware behavior (Phase 3)

## API
```typescript
const provider = new YjsSyncProvider(mesh, { 
  namespace: 'test-project' 
})
await provider.start()

const data = provider.getMap<string>('shared')
data.set('greeting', 'hello')  // Auto-syncs

provider.on('update', (update, origin) => {
  if (origin !== 'local') {
    console.log('Remote change received')
  }
})
```

## Yjs Protocol Messages
```typescript
type YjsMessage = 
  | { type: 'sync-step-1'; stateVector: Uint8Array }
  | { type: 'sync-step-2'; diff: Uint8Array; stateVector?: Uint8Array }
  | { type: 'update'; update: Uint8Array }
```

## Acceptance
- Y.Doc changes sync bidirectionally
- Multiple data types work (Map, Array, Text)
- CRDT merges concurrent edits correctly

title: Phase 2: Sudocode Integration (v0.2)
content:
# Phase 2: Sudocode Integration (v0.2)

Parent issue tracking all Phase 2 work. Goal: Specs, Issues, Relationships, and Feedback sync between sudocode instances.

## Key Decisions
- `SudocodeMeshService` lives in agentic-mesh (factor out later if needed)
- Separate CRDT state file independent from golden JSONL files
- Three test levels: unit (mock), loopback, e2e  
- All four entities: specs, issues, relationships, feedback

## Architecture

```
Golden Files (git-tracked)          CRDT State (gitignored)
.sudocode/specs.jsonl        ←→     .sudocode/mesh/crdt-state.bin
.sudocode/issues.jsonl       ←→     (Yjs document snapshot)
.sudocode/relationships.jsonl←→
.sudocode/feedback.jsonl     ←→
```

**Flow:**
1. Startup: Load JSONL → populate CRDT → sync with peers
2. Local change: Update CRDT → broadcast → debounced write to JSONL
3. Remote change: Apply to CRDT → debounced write to JSONL
4. Git pull: Detect JSONL changed → rebuild CRDT ("git wins")

## Success Criteria
- Full round-trip sync of all four entity types
- Changes persist to JSONL on both sides
- Golden JSONL files stay clean for agent interaction
- No sync loops

Implements [[s-6wrw]]

title: SudocodeMeshService skeleton and types
content:
# SudocodeMeshService Skeleton

Create the core service class that orchestrates mesh sync for sudocode entities.

## Location
`src/integrations/sudocode/` in agentic-mesh repo

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `SudocodeMeshConfig` type
- [ ] Create `SudocodeMeshService` class
- [ ] `connect()` / `disconnect()` lifecycle
- [ ] Create `YjsSyncProvider` internally with namespace
- [ ] CRDT state file management (`.sudocode/mesh/crdt-state.bin`)
- [ ] Event emitter for entity changes

## Types
```typescript
interface SudocodeMeshConfig {
  projectId: string
  projectPath: string  // Path to .sudocode/
  meshConfig: NebulaMeshConfig
}

interface SudocodeMeshService {
  connect(): Promise<void>
  disconnect(): Promise<void>
  readonly synced: boolean
  readonly peers: PeerInfo[]
  
  // Events
  on(event: 'entity:updated', handler: (type, entity, source) => void): void
  on(event: 'synced', handler: () => void): void
}
```

## Acceptance
- Service starts and connects to mesh
- Creates YjsSyncProvider with correct namespace
- Emits events on entity changes

title: Entity CRDT mapping (specs, issues, relationships, feedback)
content:
# Entity CRDT Mapping

Implement bidirectional mapping between sudocode entities and Yjs CRDT structures.

## CRDT Schema
```typescript
// Y.Doc structure
doc.getMap<SpecData>('specs')           // id -> SpecData
doc.getMap<IssueData>('issues')         // id -> IssueData
doc.getMap<RelationshipData>('relationships')  // composite-key -> RelData
doc.getMap<FeedbackData>('feedback')    // id -> FeedbackData
```

## Tasks
- [ ] Define `SpecData`, `IssueData`, `RelationshipData`, `FeedbackData` CRDT types
- [ ] Implement `specToYjs()` / `yjsToSpec()` mappers
- [ ] Implement `issueToYjs()` / `yjsToIssue()` mappers
- [ ] Implement `relationshipToYjs()` / `yjsToRelationship()` mappers
- [ ] Implement `feedbackToYjs()` / `yjsToFeedback()` mappers
- [ ] Handle optional fields and defaults
- [ ] Handle timestamps (ISO string in CRDT)

## Key Considerations
- Relationships use composite key: `${from_id}:${to_id}:${type}`
- Feedback anchored to line/text needs careful mapping
- UUIDs preserved for distributed merge
- `updated_at` set on every change

## Acceptance
- Round-trip mapping preserves all data
- Handles missing optional fields gracefully

title: JSONL sync bridge (golden file ↔ CRDT)
content:
# JSONL Sync Bridge

Bridge between golden JSONL files and CRDT state with proper reconciliation.

## Storage Layout
```
.sudocode/
├── specs.jsonl          # Golden (git-tracked)
├── issues.jsonl         # Golden (git-tracked)
├── relationships.jsonl  # Golden (git-tracked, if exists)
├── feedback.jsonl       # Golden (git-tracked, if exists)
└── mesh/                # Mesh state (gitignored)
    └── crdt-state.bin   # Yjs document snapshot
```

## Tasks
- [ ] `loadFromJSONL()` - Parse JSONL files, populate CRDT
- [ ] `saveToJSONL()` - Export CRDT state to JSONL files
- [ ] Debounced save (500ms default) to avoid thrashing
- [ ] `saveCRDTSnapshot()` - Persist Yjs state to binary file
- [ ] `loadCRDTSnapshot()` - Restore Yjs state on startup
- [ ] Add `.sudocode/mesh/` to `.gitignore`

## Startup Flow
```
1. Check if crdt-state.bin exists
2. If yes: load snapshot → sync with peers → reconcile with JSONL
3. If no: load from JSONL → create fresh CRDT → sync with peers
```

## Reconciliation ("Git Wins")
```
1. Detect JSONL changed (via hash comparison)
2. Pause incoming CRDT updates
3. Rebuild CRDT from JSONL
4. Resume sync (broadcasts rebuilt state)
```

## Acceptance
- CRDT state survives restart
- JSONL stays clean and sorted
- Git pull triggers proper reconciliation

title: Bidirectional entity sync with change detection
content:
# Bidirectional Entity Sync

Implement the sync logic that propagates changes in both directions.

## Local → Remote Flow
```
1. Local entity change (via API/CLI)
2. Update corresponding Y.Map entry
3. Yjs broadcasts update to peers
4. Debounced save to JSONL
```

## Remote → Local Flow
```
1. Receive Yjs update from peer
2. Apply to Y.Doc
3. Emit 'entity:updated' event with source='remote'
4. Debounced save to JSONL
```

## Tasks
- [ ] `syncSpec(spec)` - Push spec change to CRDT
- [ ] `syncIssue(issue)` - Push issue change to CRDT
- [ ] `syncRelationship(rel)` - Push relationship to CRDT
- [ ] `syncFeedback(fb)` - Push feedback to CRDT
- [ ] `deleteSpec(id)` / `deleteIssue(id)` / etc.
- [ ] Y.Map observers for each entity type
- [ ] Emit typed events: `spec:updated`, `issue:updated`, etc.
- [ ] Track change origin to prevent loops

## Origin Tracking
```typescript
// Tag updates with origin
doc.transact(() => {
  specs.set(id, data)
}, 'local')  // or 'remote' or 'reconcile'

// Check origin in observer
specs.observe((event) => {
  if (event.transaction.origin === 'remote') {
    // Save to JSONL
  }
})
```

## Acceptance
- Changes propagate bidirectionally
- No infinite loops
- Origin correctly identified

title: Phase 2 testing: unit, loopback, and e2e
content:
# Phase 2 Testing

Implement three levels of testing for sudocode mesh integration.

## Test Levels

### Unit Tests (Mock)
- Mock NebulaMesh and YjsSyncProvider
- Test entity mappers in isolation
- Test JSONL read/write logic
- Test reconciliation logic

### Loopback Tests
- Two SudocodeMeshService instances on localhost
- NebulaMesh connects via 127.0.0.1 (no actual Nebula)
- Test real sync flow without network complexity
- Can run in CI

### E2E Tests (Manual)
- Two machines with Nebula
- Full sudocode instances
- Document test procedure

## Tasks
- [ ] Set up vitest or jest for testing
- [ ] Unit tests for entity mappers
- [ ] Unit tests for JSONL bridge
- [ ] Loopback test harness
- [ ] Loopback test: create spec on A → appears on B
- [ ] Loopback test: edit issue on B → appears on A
- [ ] Loopback test: git reconciliation simulation
- [ ] E2E test documentation

## Loopback Test Example
```typescript
// examples/loopback-test.ts
const meshA = new NebulaMesh({
  peerId: 'peer-a',
  nebulaIp: '127.0.0.1',
  port: 7946,
  peers: [{ id: 'peer-b', nebulaIp: '127.0.0.1', port: 7947 }]
})

const meshB = new NebulaMesh({
  peerId: 'peer-b', 
  nebulaIp: '127.0.0.1',
  port: 7947,
  peers: [{ id: 'peer-a', nebulaIp: '127.0.0.1', port: 7946 }]
})

// Create SudocodeMeshService for each...
```

## Acceptance
- Unit tests pass in CI
- Loopback tests demonstrate real sync
- E2E procedure documented
